clause_quote: This Corrosion Assistance Policy applies only to vehicles with an active corrosion policy mapping recorded against the VIN.
Corrosion assistance applies only to eligible factory body panels affected by inside-out rust perforation.
If no active corrosion policy mapping exists for the VIN, the claim is not eligible under this policy.
The Corrosion Assistance Policy covers rust-through body panel perforation for 72 months from the in-service date.
A corrosion claim is within the policy period when the repair order date is on or before the recorded corrosion coverage end date.
A corrosion claim opened after the recorded policy end date is outside coverage unless referred for goodwill review.
Covered corrosion must be inside-out perforation of an eligible body panel such as a rocker panel, door hem, tailgate seam, or comparable factory panel.
Surface rust, staining, or cosmetic corrosion without panel perforation is not a covered condition.
The claim description must identify the affected panel and state that rust perforation has occurred.
The corrosion policy does not cover stone chip damage, collision-repaired panels, external damage, or improper body repair.
If the rust condition is directly linked to prior collision damage or poor repair work, the claim shall be rejected.
If the evidence of an exclusion is suggestive but not conclusive, the claim shall be referred for human review.
Every corrosion claim must include the VIN, in-service date, repair order date, affected panel, and a specific description of the perforation condition.
A corrosion claim must include a dealer inspection summary confirming that the rust originated from inside the panel and progressed to perforation.
Claims with missing mandatory documentation shall not be approved and require completion before payment.
Only repairs addressing the covered perforated panel area are eligible under the corrosion policy.
Associated refinishing and corrosion protection steps are eligible only when they are part of the covered panel repair.
Additional body work unrelated to the covered perforation area is not payable under this policy.
Labor reimbursement is evaluated against the published standard and maximum labor time for the corrosion repair code.
A claim within the published labor limit may proceed when all other corrosion coverage conditions are satisfied.
Labor time above the published maximum requires human review and cannot be auto-approved.
A corrosion claim shall be approved only when policy period, covered condition, documentation, and labor limits are all satisfied.
A corrosion claim shall be rejected when the condition is cosmetic only, caused by excluded damage, or presented outside the policy period.
A corrosion claim shall be referred when the origin of the rust or the extent of perforation is unclear.
A corrosion claim opened on the final covered day remains within coverage when all other eligibility conditions are satisfied.
If the repair order date is exactly equal to the recorded corrosion coverage end date, the claim remains within the policy period.
If the repair order date is after the recorded corrosion coverage end date, the claim is outside the policy period.
Goodwill assistance for corrosion claims may be considered only when the condition is otherwise eligible but the policy period has been exceeded by a small margin.
Goodwill requires a dealer inspection summary and a condition history that supports inside-out perforation rather than neglect or external damage.
Goodwill cannot be auto-approved and must be referred for human authorization.
